sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 7.49 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.7)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finitely generated group, and$N$a minimal normal subgroup of$G$which is an elementary abelian$p 0.2ex$-group. Is it true that either$N$is finite or the growth function for the elements of$N$with respect to a finite generating set of$G$is bounded below by an exponential function?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
